page: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It features 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core. The instance is bundled with 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing speeds, evaluated via stress-ng and Geekbench, fall into the lower tier. However, multi-core performance, including software compilation and multi-threaded decompression, reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, with read-only cached small blocks reaching 733.16 GB/sec and uncached large blocks at 69.7 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance also align with average tier expectations.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. The tradeoff of lower single-core performance is balanced by the massive 256 GB RAM capacity, making it a suitable choice for memory-intensive applications. Recommended workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that require large memory footprints but moderate CPU performance.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, paired with a substantial 256.0 GB of RAM and 524 GB of local storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks position the server in the lower tier, its multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently perform in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not require peak single-threaded compute performance. It is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v3 is an ESv3 virtual machine with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for relational databases and in-memory caching
